concerns.text: Task T034 ('Implement sensitivity sweep for clustering coefficient thresholds') fails to preserve the acceptance criterion in SC-005 and FR-008 which mandates reporting diffusion rates at '≥5 distinct clustering coefficient cutoffs'. The task description only mentions implementing 'logic' without specifying the output requirement, risking a deliverable that calculates the sweep but fails to produce the required 5-point report.

The spec's 'Edge Cases' section mandates a 'global timeout' mechanism for the batch generation of connected graphs (in addition to the 10-attempt retry). Tasks T016 (base.py) and T014 (sw.py) describe implementing retry logic and connectivity checks but omit the 'global timeout' requirement, creating a silent relaxation of the spec's failure handling constraints.

Constitution Principle VI requires metadata to record specific 'parameter values (e.g., ... edge probability, preferential-attachment parameters)' for *every* generated graph to ensure exact reconstruction. Task T019 ('Implement metadata logging') is too vague ('record algorithm params and seeds') and does not explicitly mandate the capture of these specific topology parameters, risking a violation of the Synthetic Data Transparency principle.

FR-006 requires multiple-comparison correction (Bonferroni OR Benjamini-Hochberg) for *all* tests with >1 hypothesis. Task T033 implements ANOVA and correction but the description does not explicitly ensure the Bonferroni option is available or that the correction applies to the ANOVA F-tests specifically, potentially narrowing the scope of the requirement to only regression or only BH.
